content.text: For the first five problems, show all your work and support all statements.

problem.text: Suppose that the continuous maps \(f,g:X\to Y\) are homotopic and that the continuous maps \(h,k:Y\to Z\) are homotopic. Show that \(h\circ f\) is homotopic to \(k\circ g\).
Show that the fundamental group of the circle is isomorphic to the additive group of integers. That is, \(\pi_1(S^1,1)\cong\mathbb{Z}\).
State and prove the Brouwer fixed point theorem for the unit disk, \(D^2\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Show the images of the generators of the fundamental groups of the circles do not generate the fundamental group of \(H\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Compute the fundamental group of the connected sum of a torus and a second torus \(T^2\mathbin{\#}T^2\).

content.text: Answer the following with complete definitions or statements or short proofs.

problem.text: Let \(E\) and \(B\) be topological spaces. Define what it means for a function \(p:E\to B\) to be a covering map.
If \(j:A\hookrightarrow X\) is the inclusion of a retract, then show that the induced map on fundamental groups is injective.
Let \(\alpha\) be a path in \(X\) from \(x_0\) to \(x_1\). Define the map \(\hat{\alpha}:\pi_1(X,x_0)\to\pi_1(X,x_1)\) and show that \(\hat{\alpha}\) is a group isomorphism.
Let \(S^n\) be the \(n\)-sphere. Prove that \(\pi_1(S^n)\) is trivial for \(n\ge 2\).
